summary: Tight second‑set segues, Garcia solos shine, strong Box of Rain
blurb: The March 28 1986 show delivers a powerhouse second set with seamless PITB → Franklin’s Tower → Miracle → Wheel transitions and a soaring Dear Mr. Fantasy finale. Highlights include a crisp Box of Rain, tight Playing In The Band jams and standout Garcia solos on Iko Iko, Loser and Althea. Overall the performance is solid despite a lighter Garcia‑lead song count.
review: Portland’s Cumberland County Civic Center saw the Dead roll out one of their tighter spring ’86 outings on March 28, 1986. While the first set offers solid work—most notably an inspired Box of Rain and bright renditions of Iko Iko, Loser and Althea—the real magic erupts in the second set. Starting with a fiery Playing In The Band, the band rides into Franklin’s Tower, spins through I Need A Miracle, launches into a rolling jam, then drifts through Drums/Space before surfacing on The Wheel and a majestic Dear Mr. Fantasy. Brent Mydland steals the spotlight with a tasteful keyboard showcase, and both Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann lock in tight, propelling each segue. Garcia’s health‑related limitations are barely noticeable; his solos sparkle with “amazing Bobby guitar licks” and crisp tone throughout. The audience tape flagged as the show’s best capture balances clarity and ambience, making this a must‑listen for anyone craving that mid‑80s Dead groove.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Five of seven recordings convey strong positive sentiment, highlighting the second‑set segues, Garcia solos and Brent's keyboard work; two negative entries focus only on tracking errors, not performance quality. Consistent praise across multiple sources warrants a solid positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Good audience tape with clear sound, captures the full show and receives consistent praise for balance of ambience and musical detail.

key_highlights: Second‑set segues (Playing In The Band → Franklin’s Tower → Miracle → Wheel) flow flawlessly.
Garcia delivers crisp, inventive solos on Iko Iko, Loser and Althea, earning “amazing Bobby guitar licks” praise.
Brent Mydland shines with a second‑set keyboard showcase, adding color and drive.
Box of Rain moment stands out as a heartfelt highlight in the first set.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s tone is crisp and his solos sparkle with inventive licks, especially on Iko Iko, Loser and Althea; despite health concerns he stays focused throughout.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a solid bass foundation and delivers a memorable Box of Rain moment that anchors the first set.
band_performance.Bob: Experiments with “weird things” on guitar this spring but cues Miracle flawlessly and supplies reliable rhythm work.
band_performance.Brent: Shows off a standout second‑set keyboard showcase, adding texture and drive to the jams.
band_performance.Mickey: Delivers rolling drum starts that propel each transition, keeping the segues tight and energetic.
band_performance.Billy: Pairs with Mickey to provide strong support throughout, especially during the extended PITB jam and Drum/Space sections.
